title: See the Conqu’ror mount in triumph
firstLine: See the Conqu’ror mount in triumph,
publicDomainBasis: Christopher Wordsworth (1807–1885), Arthur Seymour Sullivan (1842–1900). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
See the Conqu’ror mount in triumph,
See the King in royal state
Riding on the clouds His chariot
To His heav’nly palace gate;
Hark! The choirs of angel voices
Joyful hallelujahs sing,
And the portals high are lifted
To receive, to receive, to receive
their heav’nly King.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Who is this that comes in glory,
With the trump of jubilee?
Lord of battles, God of armies,
He has gained the victory;
He who on the cross did suffer,
He who from the grave arose,
He has vanquished sin and Satan,
He by death, He by death, He by
death has spoiled His foes.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
While He lifts His hands in blessing
He is parted from His friends;
While their eager eyes behold Him,
He upon the clouds ascends;
He who walked with God and pleased Him,
Preaching truth and doom to come,
He, our Enoch, is translated
To His home, to His home, to His
everlasting home.

sections.label: Verse 4
Verse 5
